title: Minimalist and intuitive interface
description: The mobile-application interface shall be minimalist and intuitive achieving a System Usability Scale (SUS) score >= 70.
measurable_criterion: SUS score >= 70 (equivalent to 'Good' per Bangor et al. 2008)
verification_method: User study with SUS questionnaire (n>=12 guardians)
source: Stakeholder interviews; literature (Brooke 1996; Bangor et al. 2008)

title: Non-intrusive form-factor
description: The IoT device shall be similar in form-factor and appearance to an everyday object familiar to the child (e.g. a toy) so that its presence does not disrupt the study routine.
measurable_criterion: Guardian rating >= 4/5 in the 'Statement 3' effectiveness Likert item ('The student did not perceive the presence of the device as intrusive')

title: Mains-powered operation
description: The IoT device shall be powered from the domestic mains supply given its intended fixed-location use for schoolwork.
measurable_criterion: Continuous operation >= 4 hours without battery interruption; automatic reboot within 30 s after power outage
verification_method: Endurance test (integration lab); observed field operation

title: End-to-end data encryption
description: Personal and authentication data shall be encrypted end-to-end using cryptographic mechanisms compliant with current recommendations of ISO/IEC 27001:2022.
measurable_criterion: TLS 1.3 for transport; AES-256 for data at rest; PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 with >=100000 iterations for password derivation
verification_method: Security audit; code review; TLS handshake inspection

title: Recognition latency
description: The IoT device shall communicate with the mobile application and the web service over the domestic Wi-Fi network with a mean latency below 1.5 s for the person-recognition module and below 5 s for the drowsiness-detection module.
measurable_criterion: Mean latency (person recognition) < 1.5 s; mean latency (drowsiness detection) < 5.0 s; measured over >= 15 valid sessions
verification_method: System test with instrumented latency measurement (n=16 sessions per module)
